title: machaeranthera phyllocephala var. annua
intro: Machaeranthera phyllocephala var. annua, also known as Green Mule's Ears, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its grayish-green leaves and white or purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Machaeranthera phyllocephala var. annua include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat wounds and skin conditions. Machaeranthera phyllocephala var. annua can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
